title: ICU lactate trend documentation

examination.text: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.
examination.rationale: Supports entered circulation and acute physiological observations for ICU lactate-trend documentation; it does not generate a clinical decision.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.

source.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management

exact_section.heading: Recommendation 1.3 — history, observations, circulation, respiration, skin, urine output, and source examination

assessment.text: Supports confusion, respiratory compromise, hypotension, tachycardia, oliguria, temperature abnormality, non-blanching or mottled skin, and immunosuppression as assessed risk features.
assessment.rationale: Supports clinician-assessed acute-risk context for ICU lactate-trend documentation; it does not generate a clinical decision.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports confusion, respiratory compromise, hypotension, tachycardia, oliguria, temperature abnormality, non-blanching or mottled skin, and immunosuppression as assessed risk features.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Sample time, value, units, collection context, perfusion, organ function, interventions and serial response are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, escalation or disposition is generated for ICU lactate-trend documentation.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
